title: Uragoga refracta
intro: Uragoga refracta, also known as Uva ursi or bearberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to Europe and North America, characterized by its red or yellow berries and small white flowers. The health benefits of Uragoga refracta include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract infections, diarrhea, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include arbutin, hydroquinone, and ursolic acid, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Uragoga refracta include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to support urinary tract health and immune function.
